summary: Basecamp is launching its biggest hiring drive in twenty years, starting with Rails programmers. DHH emphasizes their human-centered hiring process with no AI screening or pre-tests. The expansion is driven by HEY's unexpected rapid growth, which means they can no longer juggle both HEY and the upcoming Basecamp 4 with their current team size. He frames the growth as catching up with business reality rather than getting ahead of it, contrasting their approach with Silicon Valley's growth-at-all-costs mentality.
key_points: Basecamp uses no AI, no pre-screening companies, and no up-front tests — just human coworkers manually evaluating every application
HEY has grown far faster than any product they've ever launched, forcing a team expansion
They plan to actively develop both HEY and Basecamp 4 simultaneously rather than letting one sit idle
The hiring is framed as catching up with existing business size, not speculative growth
money_quotes: We employ no AI to sort through applications, there are no up-front tests to take, and no pre-screening from a recruiting company. Just a team of humans, consisting mainly of the candidates' future coworkers at Basecamp, manually evaluating applications.
By Silicon Valley rocket ship TOOT TOOT standards it's probably still not a lot, but it's going to be a lot for us.
Never to the point of getting ahead of the business fundamentals, but rather to catch up with the reality of the size we already have.
HEY has signed up far more customers, far faster, than any new product we've ever launched before.
We've come to the conclusion that Basecamp needs to expand a fair bit to do these two amazing, growing products we have proper justice.
key_insight: Sustainable growth means hiring to catch up with proven business demand rather than speculatively staffing ahead of it.
